 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","title":"scan extractors: fixtures + unit tests","description":"## Objective

Create fixtures and unit tests for all MVP extractors.

## Requirements

- Small fixture files per extractor (checked into tests).
- Tests assert:
  - only executable contexts are extracted
  - line numbers are correct
  - extraction is deterministic

## Critical false-positive guardrails

- Tests must explicitly include "dangerous substrings in data" cases for each extractor:
  - Shell: `FOO="rm -rf"` (must NOT extract)
  - GitHub Actions: `env: { X: "rm -rf" }` (must NOT extract)
  - Dockerfile: `ENV X="rm -rf"` (must NOT extract)
  - Makefile: `VAR = rm -rf` (must NOT extract)

## Acceptance criteria

- Extractor tests fail if we start extracting documentation strings or data-only fields.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive regression set
Ensure fixture suite includes explicit “do not flag” examples across file types:

- dangerous strings in comments
- dangerous strings in documentation fields (e.g., YAML `name:`)
- dangerous strings inside quoted arguments to non-executing tools

This is what prevents org-wide adoption from being derailed by noisy PR checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:15.323965301-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:25:20.16151379-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T19:25:20.16151379-05:00","close_reason":"Added/extended extractor false-positive regression suite in src/scan.rs: Dockerfile ENV values ignored, GitHub Actions name/env/with ignored (only steps[].run extracted), plus existing shell assignment + makefile VAR assignment guardrails. Ran fmt/check/clippy/test.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:44.040251051-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:42.964281253-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:48.062306771-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:53.158858353-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:58.256989309-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","title":"Pre-commit integration for dcg scan (staged)","description":"## Objective

Make it easy for a team to adopt `dcg scan` locally by providing a first-class pre-commit integration path.

## Background

"You should run dcg scan" is not enough. Adoption happens when:

- installation is one command
- failures are actionable
- teams can choose warn-first rollout

## Proposed UX

### The core command

- `dcg scan --staged`

### Installation helpers

Pick one (or more) pragmatic options:

1. `dcg install-pre-commit` (writes `.git/hooks/pre-commit`)
2. `dcg print-pre-commit-config` (prints snippets for popular managers)

We should not assume any specific manager.

### Configuration

- optionally read `.dcg/hooks.toml` for:
  - scan globs
  - excludes
  - fail-on policy
  - max sizes

## Safety constraints

- Default policy should be conservative:
  - `fail_on = error` (high-confidence catastrophic rules)
  - `warning` findings do not block commits by default

## Tests

- Unit test that the generated `.git/hooks/pre-commit` script is correct (string snapshot is fine).
- E2E test (shell script) that runs `dcg scan --staged` on a small fixture repo and verifies expected behavior.

## Acceptance criteria

- A user can run one command and have pre-commit scanning enabled.
- The hook can be installed idempotently.
- Documentation exists in README or `dcg scan --help` output.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Pre-commit must be warn-first by default (fail only on catastrophic rules) to avoid punishing developers for FP noise.
- The hook should print actionable allowlist guidance (rule ID) so teams can converge on low-noise configs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:49:44.718141247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:33:37.291756806-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:09.84683307-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:37.005140608-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:42.102394302-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","title":"pre-commit: install/uninstall helper (git hooks)","description":"## Objective

Implement a helper command to install a pre-commit hook that runs `dcg scan --staged`.

## Requirements

- Idempotent install.
- Clear messaging about bypass (`--no-verify`).
- Support printing configs for hook managers (optional).

## Design notes

### Do not overwrite existing hooks silently

Pre-commit hooks are user-owned and often already exist.

Proposed behavior:

- If `.git/hooks/pre-commit` does not exist: create a minimal hook that runs `dcg scan --staged`.
- If it exists and was installed by `dcg` (detect via a sentinel comment): update in-place idempotently.
- If it exists and is *not* a `dcg` hook: do **not** overwrite; instead print explicit instructions for manual integration.

### Uninstall must be safe

- Only uninstall if the hook contains the `dcg` sentinel.
- Otherwise: refuse and explain (never delete/overwrite unknown user hooks).

### Exit codes / messaging

- The installed hook should:
  - surface scan output
  - fail the commit when `--fail-on` threshold is met
  - mention bypass path (`git commit --no-verify`) but encourage allowlisting / fixing

## Test plan

- E2E test in a temp git repo:
  - install when hook absent
  - install again (idempotent)
  - uninstall
  - attempt uninstall when non-dcg hook exists (should refuse)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg scan install-pre-commit` installs a working `.git/hooks/pre-commit` hook when none exists.
- Running install twice is a no-op (idempotent) and never duplicates hook lines.
- `dcg scan uninstall-pre-commit` removes only hooks it installed (sentinel-based) and refuses to touch other hooks.
- Hook messaging is clear about:
  - what failed
  - how to fix/allowlist
  - how to bypass (`--no-verify`) with explicit risk warning
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:17:55.636908818-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T19:48:36.091372303-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T19:48:36.091372303-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented `dcg scan install-pre-commit` + `uninstall-pre-commit` (sentinel-based, idempotent, refuses unknown hooks), refactored scan CLI to support optional subcommands, and added temp-git-repo unit tests for install/uninstall + CLI parsing/conflict coverage.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:19.453653841-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:10.086282153-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:15.185035321-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","title":"pre-commit: .dcg/hooks.toml config parsing","description":"## Objective

Support a project config file that controls `dcg scan` behavior for pre-commit usage.

This exists so teams can adopt scanning without requiring everyone to remember long CLI flag sets.

## Proposed config file

- Path: `.dcg/hooks.toml`
- Scope: repository (committed, code-reviewed)

## Config goals

- Make rollout conservative and low-noise by default.
- Provide a single place to encode:
  - what to scan
  - what to exclude
  - how to redact
  - when to fail

## Suggested schema (v1)

```toml
[scan]
fail_on = "error"           # error|warning|none
format = "json"             # pretty|json (pre-commit usually uses pretty)
max_file_size = 1000000
max_findings = 50
redact = "quoted"           # none|quoted|aggressive
truncate = 200

[scan.paths]
include = ["scripts/**", ".github/workflows/**", "Dockerfile", "Makefile"]
exclude = ["target/**", "node_modules/**"]
```

## Validation behavior

- Unknown keys produce warnings (do not crash hook).
- Invalid values produce clear errors in `dcg scan` / `dcg doctor`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg scan --staged` (pre-commit integration) can load `.dcg/hooks.toml` automatically.
- CLI flags override config file values (explicit beats implicit).
- Config parsing failures are actionable.

## Tests

- Golden tests for config parsing:
  - valid config
  - unknown keys warnings
  - invalid enum values
- Integration test: config affects include/exclude and redact behavior deterministically.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:00.729320573-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:30:33.206219139-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:24.545211146-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.281979873-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:25.375217044-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:00.729320573-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T20:00:01.847120088-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T20:00:01.847120088-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented repo-local scan config via .dcg/hooks.toml: added parser with unknown-key warnings + actionable errors, wired automatic loading (git toplevel) into dcg scan, refactored scan CLI flags to be optional so config can supply defaults, and added unit tests for parsing + merge/override behavior.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:24.545211146-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.281979873-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:25.375217044-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","title":"pre-commit: E2E fixture repo + detailed logging","description":"## Objective

Add an end-to-end test that:

- creates a small fixture repo
- stages files with known findings
- runs `dcg scan --staged`
- asserts output summary and exit code

Logging must include per finding:

- file:line
- extracted command
- rule id
- reason

## Design notes

- The fixture repo should include at least:
  - a safe file (no findings)
  - a file with an executable destructive command in an executed context
  - a file with a *data-only* mention of a destructive string (should not be flagged)
- The test should assert both:
  - correctness (findings exist where expected)
  - ergonomics (output is actionable and stable)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test creates a temp git repo, stages files, runs `dcg scan --staged`, and asserts:
  - exit code matches `--fail-on` policy
  - output contains stable rule IDs and file:line locations
  - extracted commands are shown (redacted/truncated per policy)
- Test includes at least one negative fixture ensuring data-only mentions do not produce findings.
- Test output includes enough logging to debug failures without re-running locally.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:05.825341465-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:54:10.738525115-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:29.635035168-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:30.532356101-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:35.79204859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","title":"pre-commit: docs + recommended rollout","description":"## Objective

Document how to adopt `dcg scan` in a pre-commit workflow **safely** (low-noise rollout that preserves developer/agent velocity).

## Required doc sections

### 1) What this is (and is not)

- `dcg` hook protects *interactive execution*.
- `dcg scan` protects repos by scanning *executable contexts* in files.
- Emphasize: extractor-based scanning (NOT naive grep) to avoid false positives.

### 2) Recommended rollout plan (warn-first)

- Start with:
  - narrow include set (workflows + Dockerfiles + Makefiles)
  - `--fail-on error` only
  - warnings informational for 1–2 weeks

- Expand gradually:
  - add `.sh` scripts
  - add additional extractors (compose, Terraform, GitLab CI, package.json)

### 3) How to interpret findings

- Meaning of `decision` and `severity`.
- How to read `rule_id` and why it is stable.

### 4) How to fix a finding

- Run `dcg explain "..."` for the extracted command.
- Prefer changing code to safer alternatives.
- If it is a false positive:
  - use `dcg allow` / `dcg allowlist add ...` (project or user)
  - explain allowlist safety constraints (expiration, risk_ack)

### 5) Privacy / secrets

- Recommend redaction defaults.
- Avoid printing full extracted commands in CI logs.

### 6) Example configurations

- `.dcg/hooks.toml` example for staged scanning.
- Example pre-commit hook snippet (and optional managers: husky/lefthook).

## Acceptance criteria

- A user can adopt scan-precommit without reading source.
- Docs explicitly warn against turning on warning-as-fail immediately.
- Docs show exactly how to resolve false positives safely.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:10.920878487-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:30:51.834974021-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:34.725455653-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:41.044919711-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.4.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:46.278526462-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","title":"CI integration for dcg scan (PR diff scanning + PR summary)","description":"## Objective

Provide a "team-wide" safety net by running `dcg scan` in CI for pull requests.

## Background

Local hooks are not always installed or are bypassed (`--no-verify`). CI is where:

- the org can enforce safety rules
- results can be reviewed centrally
- rollouts can start in warn-only mode (avoid blocking velocity)

## Proposed MVP (GitHub Actions)

### 1) Workflow snippet (no marketplace action yet)

- A documented GitHub Actions workflow that:
  - checks out full history (diff computation)
  - builds `dcg` (or downloads release artifact later)
  - runs `dcg scan --git-diff <base>...HEAD --format json --fail-on error`
  - uploads the JSON artifact

### 2) Human-readable PR summary

- Render a concise Markdown summary to the job log (and optionally as a PR comment):
  - top N findings, grouped by file
  - per finding: line, extracted command (truncated), rule id, reason, suggestion
  - totals by severity (error/warn/info)

### 3) Rollout model

- Default recommendation:
  - start with `--fail-on error` only
  - treat warnings as advisory for 1-2 weeks
  - tighten after allowlists are created

## Output requirements

- JSON schema must be stable:
  - include `file`, `line`, `extractor_id`, `extracted_command`, `decision`, `rule_id`, `reason`, `suggestion?`
- CI mode should have deterministic ordering.

## Security/privacy

- Provide a `--redact` option (or config) for commands that might contain secrets.
- CI should avoid printing entire extracted commands if they are very long.

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg scan --git-diff ...` handles empty diffs cleanly (0 findings, exit 0).
- A workflow snippet exists in docs or templates and is proven to run in this repo.
- When errors exist, CI fails with an actionable summary.
- When warnings exist (and `--fail-on error`), CI passes but prints the warnings clearly.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- CI should default to diff-only scanning and warn-first rollout.
- PR comments must clearly show extracted executed commands so reviewers can judge signal.

### Tests
- `git_safety_guard-scan.5.3` covers `--git-diff` integration behavior.
- Add at least one end-to-end CI fixture that includes:
  - a true positive in an executed context
  - a data-only dangerous string that must not be flagged","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:31.299265569-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:28:51.436024163-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:56:40.548508411-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:47.19945344-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:52.295002514-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
